text: Open Source Intel SecWar Hegseth says the U.S. kept a live coordination call running for nearly 48 hours for the American airman stranded in Iran. “The top of it read, 45 hours and 56 minutes. For 45 hours and 56 minutes, we held that call open for coordination!” “From the moment our pilots went down, our mission was unblinking. The call never dropped, the meeting never stopped, the planning never ceased.” “The President demanded rapid, decisive action.” “He backed our military with the full weight of his resolve, refusing to let enemy propaganda or tactical setbacks deter us.” tweet

WarTranslated The "Minudobreniya" chemical plant in Rossosh, Voronezh region, has been attacked. The OSINT community CyberBoroshno confirms that the main fertilizer storage facility is on fire, posing a risk of a massive explosion. This plant is a major producer of ammonium nitrate, which is used in the manufacture of both explosives and fertilizers. tweet
Iranian missiles reportedly hit Al Jubail in Saudi Arabia, causing a large fire. Al Jubail is the Middle East’s largest industrial hub, home to major petrochemical and energy facilities.

Iranian Light crude became more expensive than Brent (~+$1/bbl), a rare shift from its usual discounted price. This happened because war-driven supply shortages and limited sanctions relief made Iranian oil unusually valuable and in high demand.

The kill vehicle from a U.S. THAAD missile interceptor was recovered in southern Syria (Suwayda) after a recent interception. This component is the non-explosive part that destroys targets by direct high-speed impact (“hit-to-kill”). It likely fell back to Syria intact after completing or failing its mission, which is possible since THAAD carries no warhead.

Three U.S. personnel were injured during the Iran rescue mission: the downed F-15E weapons officer and two helicopter crew members hit by ground fire. Rescue helicopters came under intense small-arms fire, with one aircraft taking multiple hits, while the injured officer managed to move and signal his location despite severe wounds. Source: AP

⚡️ 🇮🇶 ☀️ A drone strike has hit the headquarters of the Peshmarga Brigade 6 and the Staff College in Chwarqurna, located in the independent administration of Raparin, KRI. The base had not been evacuated and many people were inside at the late hour, likely asleep. Casualties have been reported. @wfwitness

They’re located roughly 20…" During a high-risk rescue mission inside Iran, the U.S. used heavy force, dropping around 100 large bombs from B-1 bombers to secure the area, destroy roads, and keep Iranian forces away from the extraction site. Israel has approved a new list of Iranian energy and infrastructure targets in case diplomacy fails, but is waiting for U.S. approval before acting. Israeli officials are skeptical a deal will work and are pushing for any ceasefire to require Iran to fully stop uranium enrichment and give up its enriched stockpile. Source: CNN

An Iranian drone strike on a U.S. air base in Kuwait injured 15 American personnel, most of whom have already returned to duty. Overall, 373 U.S. service members have been injured in the operation so far, with the majority back in service and five listed as seriously wounded. Source: CBS
